notes: Windows + Cursor CLI (agent) + Ralph method (no upstream bash script). One iteration handles one story.

userStories.title: Add date and color marker before read-later article titles
userStories.description: In the read-later article list, render a date label and color marker box to the left of each article title using the same logic as the Feed article list.
acceptanceCriteria: Each read-later item shows date and color marker box before the title.
Date rendering logic matches Feed article list behavior.
Color marker logic matches Feed article list behavior.
Visual ordering is date + color marker + title from left to right.
Relevant frontend tests are updated or added, and pass.

userStories.title: Create Linux/macOS startup script (start.sh)
userStories.description: Create scripts/start.sh as the Unix equivalent of scripts/start.cmd. The script should support the same features: one-click start of backend + frontend, optional port arguments, auto-detect venv, bind to all interfaces for LAN access.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/start.sh exists and is executable (chmod +x).
Script accepts optional [backend_port] [frontend_port] arguments with same defaults as start.cmd.
Script auto-detects and activates backend/.venv if present.
Script starts backend (uvicorn) and frontend (npm run dev) in background or separate terminals.
Script works on both Linux and macOS (bash-compatible, no bashisms that break on macOS).
Script prints startup URLs and instructions.
README.md updated to reference start.sh for macOS/Linux.

userStories.title: Create Linux/macOS CI check script (ci-check.sh)
userStories.description: Create scripts/ci-check.sh as the Unix equivalent of scripts/ci-check.cmd. The script should run the same quality gates: ruff, black, mypy, pytest for backend; lint, typecheck, test:ui, test:e2e for frontend.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ci-check.sh exists and is executable.
Script runs all backend checks: ruff check, black --check, mypy, pytest.
Script runs all frontend checks: npm run lint, typecheck, test:ui, test:e2e.
Script exits with non-zero code on any failure.
Script works on both Linux and macOS.
docs/developer-guide.md updated to mention ci-check.sh for Unix systems.

userStories.title: Update developer-guide.md with Linux/macOS instructions
userStories.description: Expand docs/developer-guide.md to include setup, run, and quality gate commands for Linux and macOS alongside the existing Windows instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: Local setup section shows both Windows (.bat) and Unix (.sh) commands side by side.
Local run section references both start.cmd and start.sh.
Quality gate section references both ci-check.cmd and ci-check.sh.
Manual start instructions (without scripts) included for both platforms.
venv activation syntax shown for both Windows and Unix.

userStories.title: Update README.md installation instructions for cross-platform
userStories.description: Update README.md to show installation and quick start commands for all three platforms (Windows, macOS, Linux) instead of Windows-only .bat syntax.
acceptanceCriteria: Installation section shows commands for Windows (cmd), macOS/Linux (bash).
venv activation shown for both platforms: .venv\Scripts\activate.bat vs source .venv/bin/activate.
Quick start section references both start.cmd (Windows) and start.sh (Unix).
Manual start commands included for users who prefer not to use the scripts.

userStories.title: Create Linux deployment guide (deployment-linux.md)
userStories.description: Create docs/deployment-linux.md with production deployment instructions for Linux servers, covering systemd service setup, reverse proxy (nginx), and best practices.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/deployment-linux.md exists with complete deployment guide.
Guide covers systemd unit files for backend service.
Guide covers nginx reverse proxy configuration.
Guide covers static frontend hosting (nginx or separate).
Guide includes environment variable configuration.
Guide includes data directory permissions setup.
Guide includes pre-run checklist (healthz, logs, permissions).
docs/README.md updated to link to the new deployment guide.

userStories.title: Create systemd service unit files
userStories.description: Create ready-to-use systemd unit files for running RSSight backend as a Linux service, with optional frontend service or static hosting.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/systemd/rssight-backend.service exists with proper unit file.
Service file uses correct ExecStart for uvicorn.
Service file includes proper User, WorkingDirectory, Environment settings.
Service file includes Restart=on-failure for reliability.
Optional: scripts/systemd/rssight-frontend.service for dev-mode frontend.
Installation instructions included in deployment-linux.md.

userStories.title: Create nginx configuration template
userStories.description: Create a sample nginx configuration file for reverse-proxying to RSSight backend and serving static frontend files.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/nginx/rssight.conf exists with sample configuration.
Config includes upstream block for backend API.
Config includes location blocks for /api proxy and static frontend.
Config includes WebSocket support if needed for future features.
Config includes recommended security headers.
Installation instructions included in deployment-linux.md.

userStories.title: Update AGENTS.md platform rules for cross-platform
userStories.description: Update AGENTS.md section 3 (Platform) to acknowledge cross-platform support and provide guidance for agents working on Linux/macOS.
acceptanceCriteria: Platform section no longer states 'Target Windows' exclusively.
Section acknowledges Windows, macOS, and Linux as supported platforms.
Guidance provided for when to use .cmd vs .sh scripts.
Guidance for shell command differences (cmd vs bash) included.
Rule added: when adding new scripts, create both .cmd and .sh versions.

userStories.title: Add cross-platform CI workflow (GitHub Actions)
userStories.description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml to run quality gates on Linux (and optionally Windows/macOS) using GitHub Actions.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml exists with working CI configuration.
Workflow runs on ubuntu-latest (primary).
Workflow installs Python 3.12+ and Node 20+.
Workflow runs backend checks: ruff, black, mypy, pytest.
Workflow runs frontend checks: lint, typecheck, test:ui.
Workflow triggers on push and pull_request.
Optional: matrix build for multiple OS (ubuntu, windows, macos).

userStories.title: Create Docker support for Linux deployment
userStories.description: Create Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml for containerized deployment on Linux servers.
acceptanceCriteria: Dockerfile exists for building backend image.
Dockerfile uses multi-stage build for smaller image.
Dockerfile properly handles Python dependencies and uvicorn.
docker-compose.yml exists with backend and frontend services.
docker-compose.yml includes volume mount for data/ persistence.
docker-compose.yml includes proper networking between services.
docs/deployment-linux.md updated with Docker deployment option.

userStories.title: Add Makefile for cross-platform development commands
userStories.description: Create a Makefile with common development targets (install, dev, test, lint, build) that work on Linux/macOS and provide a consistent interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Makefile exists at repository root.
make install: installs backend and frontend dependencies.
make dev: starts development servers.
make test: runs all tests.
make lint: runs all linters.
make build: builds frontend for production.
make clean: removes generated files and caches.
Makefile documented in developer-guide.md.
